message:
fix(messaging): harden NATS/JetStream JSON serialization against injection

Add json_escape() helpers to both nats.rs and jetstream.rs that properly
escape double quotes, backslashes, and control characters (U+0000..U+001F)
when embedding user-supplied strings in hand-built JSON payloads.

Previously, stream names, subject filters, consumer names, durable names,
NATS client names, usernames, passwords, and auth tokens were interpolated
directly into JSON strings without escaping. A value containing a literal
double quote would produce malformed JSON or allow injection of arbitrary
JSON keys/values.

Also fixes extract_json_string_simple() (jetstream) and extract_json_string()
(nats) to respect backslash escapes when scanning for the closing quote,
preventing incorrect truncation of values that contain escaped characters.

Additional JetStream fixes in this changeset:
- publish_with_id() now returns an explicit error instead of silently
  constructing a broken API request, since our NATS client lacks header
  support required for Nats-Msg-Id deduplication.
- Consumer pull loop switches from timeout() to timeout_at() with a
  pre-computed deadline to avoid drift across iterations.
- ByStartSequence deliver policy now emits opt_start_seq in consumer JSON.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
